003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.jcs;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022
023import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.*;
024import org.apache.juneau.marshall.marshaller.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
027
028/**
029 * Serializes POJO models to canonical JSON per RFC 8785 (JCS).
030 *
031 * <h5 class='topic'>Media types</h5>
032 * <p>
033 *    Produces media type: <bc>application/jcs+json</bc>
034 * </p>
035 *
036 * <h5 class='topic'>Description</h5>
037 * <p>
038 *    JCS (JSON Canonicalization Scheme) produces a deterministic, byte-for-byte canonical
039 *    representation of JSON, enabling reliable cryptographic operations such as hashing and
040 *    digital signing. All canonicalization rules are defined in
041 *    <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8785">RFC 8785</a>.
042 * </p>
043 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
044 *    <li>No whitespace between tokens.
045 *    <li>Object properties sorted by UTF-16 code unit order, applied recursively to all nested objects.
046 *    <li>Numbers serialized using ECMAScript-compatible rules: shortest round-trip representation,
047 *        no trailing zeros, lowercase <code>e</code>, positive exponent sign included (e.g. <code>1e+30</code>).
048 *    <li>Negative zero serialized as <code>0</code>.
049 *    <li>Non-ASCII characters emitted as literal UTF-8 bytes (no unnecessary unicode escape sequences).
050 *    <li>Array element order is preserved (not sorted).
051 * </ul>
052 *
053 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
054 * <p class='bjava'>
055 *    <jc>// Create a bean.</jc>
056 *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jk>new</jk> MyBean().name(<js>"Alice"</js>).age(30);
057 *
058 *    <jc>// Serialize to canonical JSON.</jc>
059 *    String <jv>json</jv> = JcsSerializer.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.serialize(<jv>bean</jv>);
060 *
061 *    <jc>// Or use the Jcs marshaller convenience method.</jc>
062 *    String <jv>json</jv> = Jcs.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>bean</jv>);
063 * </p>
064 *
065 * <h5 class='figure'>Example output (bean with name/age):</h5>
066 * <p class='bjson'>
067 *    {<jok>"age"</jok>:<jov>30</jov>,<jok>"name"</jok>:<jov>"Alice"</jov>}
068 * </p>
069 *
070 * <h5 class='figure'>Example output with nested object:</h5>
071 * <p class='bjson'>
072 *    {<jok>"address"</jok>:{<jok>"city"</jok>:<jov>"Denver"</jov>,<jok>"zip"</jok>:<jov>"80201"</jov>},<jok>"name"</jok>:<jov>"Alice"</jov>}
073 * </p>
074 *
075 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
076 *    <li class='note'>This class is thread safe and reusable.
077 *    <li class='note'>
078 *       JCS output is valid JSON and can be parsed using the standard {@link JsonParser}.
079 *       The {@link Jcs} marshaller pairs this serializer with {@link JsonParser} for full round-trip support.
080 *    <li class='note'>
081 *       {@link java.math.BigDecimal} and {@link java.math.BigInteger} values beyond IEEE 754 double
082 *       precision range will lose precision or throw a {@link SerializeException}.
083 *       This is a spec-level constraint defined by RFC 8785.
084 *    <li class='note'>
085 *       {@link Double#NaN}, {@link Double#POSITIVE_INFINITY}, and {@link Double#NEGATIVE_INFINITY}
086 *       are not permitted and will throw a {@link SerializeException}.
087 *    <li class='note'>
088 *       Strings containing lone UTF-16 surrogate code units will throw {@link SerializeException}.
089 *    <li class='note'>
090 *       Uses <code>application/jcs+json</code> to avoid content-negotiation collision with
091 *       {@link JsonSerializer} (<code>application/json</code>). Request explicitly via
092 *       <c>Accept: application/jcs+json</c> for canonical output.
093 * </ul>
094 *
095 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
096 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8785">RFC 8785 — JSON Canonicalization Scheme</a>
097 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Jcs">JCS topic</a>
098 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Jcs}
099 *    <li class='jc'>{@link JsonParser}
100 * </ul>
101 */
102@SuppressWarnings({
103   "java:S110", // Inheritance depth acceptable for this class hierarchy
104   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase naming convention
105   "resource" // Closeable resources are owned by the caller's serializer session; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
106})
107public class JcsSerializer extends JsonSerializer {
108
109   private static final String ARG_copyFrom = "copyFrom";
110
111   /**
112    * Builder class.
113    */
114   public static class Builder extends JsonSerializer.Builder<Builder> {
115
116      /**
117       * Constructor, default settings for JCS canonical output.
118       */
119      protected Builder() {
120         super();
121         produces("application/jcs+json");
122         accept("application/jcs+json");
123         quoteChar('"');
124         useWhitespace(false);
125      }
126
127      /**
128       * Copy constructor.
129       *
130       * @param copyFrom The builder to copy from.
131       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
132       */
133      protected Builder(Builder copyFrom) {
134         super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_copyFrom, copyFrom));
135      }
136
137      /**
138       * Copy constructor.
139       *
140       * @param copyFrom The serializer to copy from.
141       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
142       */
143      protected Builder(JcsSerializer copyFrom) {
144         super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_copyFrom, copyFrom));
145      }
146
147      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
148      public Builder copy() {
149         return new Builder(this);
150      }
151
152      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
153      public JcsSerializer build() {
154         return new JcsSerializer(this);
155      }
156   }
157
158   /** Default serializer. */
159   public static final JcsSerializer DEFAULT = new JcsSerializer(create());
160
161   /**
162    * Creates a new builder for this object.
163    *
164    * @return A new builder.
165    */
166   public static Builder create() {
167      return new Builder();
168   }
169
170   /**
171    * Constructor.
172    *
173    * @param builder The builder for this object.
174    */
175   public JcsSerializer(Builder builder) {
176      super(builder);
177   }
178
179   @Override /* Overridden from Context */
180   public Builder copy() {
181      return new Builder(this);
182   }
183
184   @Override /* Overridden from JsonSerializer */
185   public JcsSerializerSession.Builder createSession() {
186      return JcsSerializerSession.create(this);
187   }
188
189   /**
190    * Convenience delegator (default session args) for the raw-JSON token writer.  Real impl on
191    * {@link JcsSerializerSession#serializeTokens(Object)}.
192    *
193    * <p>
194    * <b>Note:</b> the cursor's structural methods ({@link TokenWriter#startObject() startObject},
195    * {@link TokenWriter#fieldName(String) fieldName}, {@link TokenWriter#string(String) string}, ...)
196    * produce ordinary JSON; they do <em>not</em> apply the JCS canonicalization rules (sorted
197    * keys, normalized number formatting, etc.) that {@link #serialize(Object)} produces.  The
198    * {@link TokenWriter#object(Object) object(Object)} bridge is <b>disabled</b> on this writer.
199    * Use {@link #serialize(Object)} for canonical output.
200    *
201    * @param output The output.
202    * @return A new {@link JsonTokenWriter} with {@code object(...)} disabled.
203    * @throws IOException If the output type is not supported or could not be opened.
204    */
205   @Override /* TokenWritable */
206   public TokenWriter serializeTokens(Object output) throws IOException {
207      return getSession().serializeTokens(output);
208   }
209
210   /**
211    * Convenience delegator (default session args) for a record writer that emits canonical JCS
212    * for the value passed to {@link RecordWriter#write(Object) write(...)}.  Real impl on
213    * {@link JcsSerializerSession#serializeRecords(Object)}.
214    *
215    * @param output The output.
216    * @return A new {@link RecordWriter}.
217    * @throws IOException If the output type is not supported or could not be opened.
218    */
219   @Override /* RecordWritable */
220   public RecordWriter serializeRecords(Object output) throws IOException {
221      return getSession().serializeRecords(output);
222   }
223
224   @Override /* RecordWritable */
225   public boolean isRecordStreaming() { return false; }
226}
